question_text: A hydraulic system uses two pistons of different sizes. The small piston (area 0.01 m²) is pushed with a force of 500 N. The large piston (area 0.5 m²) is connected to lift a car. The pressure created by the small piston is transmitted through the fluid to the large piston. How much force can the large piston exert?
explanation: Pressure in the fluid is the same everywhere: P = 500/0.01 = 50,000 Pa. On the large piston, force = pressure × area = 50,000 × 0.5 = 25,000 N. This shows how hydraulics can multiply force using different piston sizes.
